{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-46639", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:H/VI:H/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-693", "name": "Protection Mechanism Failure"}], "description": "### Description

The object-destructuring assignment syntax introduced in Twig 3.24.0 generates a call to `CoreExtension::getAttribute()` with the `$sandboxed` argument hardcoded to `false`, regardless of whether a `SandboxExtension` is active. This permanently disables the sandbox's property and method policy checks for every destructuring expression.

`ObjectDestructuringSetBinary::compile()` emits:

```php
CoreExtension::getAttribute($this->env, $this->source, ..., \Twig\Template::ANY_CALL, false, false, false, ...);
//                                                                                ^^^^^
//                                                                       sandbox check never runs
```

Whereas `GetAttrExpression::compile()` correctly passes `$env->hasExtension(SandboxExtension::class)`.

An attacker with write access to a sandboxed Twig template can read any public property or invoke any public getter on objects passed to the template engine, bypassing `SecurityPolicy` restrictions. The exploit requires only the `{% do %}` tag to be in `allowedTags`, which is a common configuration.

### Resolution

The destructuring compiler now forwards the active sandbox flag to `getAttribute()` so property/method allowlists are enforced.

### Credits

Twig would like to thank Anvil Secure in collaboration with Claude and Anthropic Research for reporting and fixing the issue.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00351, "percentile": 0.27334}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-mm6w-gr99-p3jj", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-21T21:30:49Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-mm6w-gr99-p3jj", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-mm6w-gr99-p3jj"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-46639"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-05-21T21:30:49Z", "references": ["https://github.com/twigphp/Twig/security/advisories/GHSA-mm6w-gr99-p3jj", "https://github.com/FriendsOfPHP/security-advisories/blob/master/twig/twig/CVE-2026-46639.yaml", "https://symfony.com/cve-2026-46639", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-mm6w-gr99-p3jj"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/twigphp/Twig/security-advisories/GHSA-mm6w-gr99-p3jj", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/twigphp/Twig", "summary": "Twig: Sandbox property and method bypass via object-destructuring assignment", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-21T21:30:50Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-mm6w-gr99-p3jj", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "3.26.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "twig/twig"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 3.24.0, < 3.26.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Twig: Sandbox property and method bypass via object-destructuring assignment
